title: Ballard Power Systems Q4 2024 Earnings Call Highlights
text: Ballard Power Systems faced significant challenges in Q4 2024, with revenue declining 42% year-over-year to $24.5 million. Full-year 2024 revenue fell 32% to $69.7 million. The company posted a negative 13% gross margin in Q4, though this represented a 9 percentage point improvement from the prior year quarter. Management implemented aggressive cost-cutting measures with operating expenses for 2024 totaling $161.3 million, including a $17 million restructuring charge. The company projects 2025 operating expenses between $100-120 million, a reduction of approximately 30% from 2024 levels. Capital expenditures are expected to decline roughly 38% to $15-25 million. Despite revenue headwinds, Ballard achieved record order intake of $113 million in 2024. The backlog reached $173.5 million by year-end, representing a 41% sequential increase. Fuel cell engine shipments grew 30% to over 660 units. The company maintains strong liquidity with approximately $604 million in cash and zero bank debt. CEO Randy MacEwen noted that adoption timelines in the truck sector have materially pushed out, with industry consolidation creating uncertainty. The company deferred its Texas gigafactory investment decision to 2026 pending clearer market signals.
